node.statement: (Single-constraint case — Weis-Shirokov Theorem 2). Let K be a convex set in a locally convex space, f : K → R ∪ {+∞} a lower semicontinuous generalized affine map, and C a sublevel set C = {x ∈ K : f(x) ≤ β} or level set C = {x ∈ K : f(x) = β}. For any x ∈ C: dim(F_K(x)) ≤ dim(F_C(x)) + 1. Proof outline: If y ∈ F_K(x), then we can write x in a convex combination involving y. By affineness of f and the constraint f(x) = β (or ≤ β), the constraint propagates to y. A careful analysis of the face structure shows the face F_K(x) is contained in a set whose dimension exceeds dim(F_C(x)) by at most 1 (the one additional 'direction' being the direction along which f varies). See Weis-Shirokov 2021, proof of Theorem 2.
